title: Analog-to-digital converter, measurement arrangement and method for analog-to-digital conversion
abstract: An analog-to-digital converter (10) comprises a first and a second sampling capacitor (24, 25), a first integrator (26), a first and a second input switch (31, 32) coupling a first input terminal (11) and a common mode terminal (39) to a first electrode of the first sampling capacitor (24), a third and a fourth input switch (33, 34) coupling a second input terminal (12) and the common mode terminal (39)to a first electrode of the second sampling capacitor (25), a fifth and a sixth input switch (35, 36) coupling a second electrode of the first sampling capacitor (24) to an amplifier common mode terminal (40)and the first integrator input (27), and a seventh and an eighth input switch (37, 38) coupling a second electrode of the second sampling capacitor (25) to the amplifier common mode terminal (40) and the second integrator input (28).
claims:
Claims Claims 1. Analog-to-digital converter, comprising a first and a second sampling capacitor (24, 25), - a first integrator (26) having a first and a second integrator input (27, 28), a first and a second input switch (31 , 32) that couple a first input terminal (11) and a common mode terminal (39) to a first electrode of the first sampling capacitor (24), a third and a fourth input switch (33, 34) that couple a second input terminal (12) and the common mode terminal (39) to a first electrode of the second sampling capacitor (25), a fifth and a sixth input switch (35, 36) that couple a second electrode of the first sampling capacitor (24) to an amplifier common mode terminal (40) and to the first integrator input (27), and a seventh and an eighth input switch (37, 38) that couple a second electrode of the second sampling capacitor (25) to the amplifier common mode terminal (40) and to the second integrator input (28). 2. Analog-to-digital converter according to claim 1, wherein the first integrator (26) comprises - an amplifier (41) having a first amplifier input (42) connected to the first integrator input (27) and a second amplifier input (43) connected to the second integrator input (28), a first integrating capacitor (44) coupled to the first amplifier input (42) and to a first amplifier output of the amplifier (41), and - a second integrating capacitor (45) coupled to the second amplifier input (43) and to a second amplifier output of the amplifier (41). 3. Analog-to-digital converter according to claim 2, wherein the amplifier (41) is connected to the amplifier common mode terminal (40). 4. Analog-to-digital converter according to claim 2 or 3, wherein the analog-to-digital converter (10) comprises 

 a second integrator (46) coupled on its input side to the first and the second amplifier output, a comparator (47) coupled on its input side to an output side of the second integrator (46) and - a filter (15) coupled on its input side to the output side of the second integrator (46). 5. Analog-to-digital converter according to one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the analog-to-digital converter (10) is configured as a sigma-delta modulator. 6. Analog-to-digital converter according to one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the analog-to-digital converter (10) comprises a first clock generator (18) having a first output connected to the fifth and the seventh input switch (35, 37) and a second output connected to the sixth and eighth input switch (36, 38) and - a second clock generator (19) having a first output (102) connected to at least one of the first and the third input switch (31, 33) and a second output connected to at least one of the second and the fourth input switch (32, 34). 7. Analog-to-digital converter according to claim 6, wherein the first and the second clock generator (18, 19) are coupled such that the first, third, fifth and seventh input switch (31, 33, 35, 37) receive a first logical signal (Î¦1, Î¦Î) and the second, fourth, sixth and eighth input switch (32, 34, 36, 38) receive a second logical signal (Î¦2, Î¦2'). 8. Analog-to-digital converter according to claim 6 or 7, wherein the first to the fourth input switch (31 to 34), the first and the second sampling capacitor (24, 25) and the second clock generator (19) are configured to operate at a higher voltage level than the fifth to the eighth input switch (35 to 38) and the first clock generator (18). 9. Analog-to-digital converter according to one of claims 6 to 8, wherein the second clock generator (19) comprises 

 an input stage (104) connected on its input side to a first input (101) of the second clock generator (19), an intermediate stage (105) and an output stage (106) connected on its output side to the first output (102) of the second clock generator (19), wherein the intermediate stage (105) couples the input stage (104) to the output stage (106). 10. Analog-to-digital converter according to one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the analog-to-digital converter (10) comprises a resistive voltage divider (85) having a first resistor (86) coupling the first input terminal (11) to the common mode terminal (39) and a second resistor (87) coupling the common mode terminal (39) to the second input terminal (12). 11. Measurement arrangement, comprising the analog-to-digital converter (10) according to one of claims 1 to 10, a first cell (75) and a coupler (90) coupling a first terminal (76) of the first cell (75) to the first input terminal (11) and a second terminal (77) of the first cell (75) to the second input terminal (12). 12. Measurement arrangement according to claim 11, wherein the measurement arrangement (60) comprises at least a second cell (79, 80) and the coupler (90) selectively couples - the first terminal (76) of the first cell (75) to the first input terminal (11) and the second terminal (77) of the first cell (75) to the second input terminal (12) or a first terminal of the second cell (79) to the first input terminal (11) and a second terminal of the second cell (79) to the second input terminal (12). 13. Measurement arrangement, comprising the analog-to-digital converter (10) according to one of claims 1 to 10 and a resistive sensor (61) having a first terminal connected to the first input terminal (11) and a second terminal connected to the second input terminal (12). 14. Measurement arrangement according to claim 13, wherein the resistive sensor (61) comprises a first and a second resistive element (62, 63) and a tap, and wherein the first resistive element (62) couples the first terminal of the resistive sensor (61) to the tap of the resistive sensor (61), the second resistive element (63) couples the tap of the resistive sensor (61) to the second terminal of the resistive sensor (61) and the tap of the resistive sensor (61) is connected to the common mode terminal (39). 15. Method for analog-to-digital conversion, comprising in a first phase (PA), sampling a first difference (AVP) between a first input voltage (VINP) and an amplifier common mode voltage (VCM) by a first sampling capacitor (24) and sampling a second difference (AVN) between a second input voltage (VENN) and the amplifier common mode voltage (VCM) by a second sampling capacitor (25), and in a second phase (PB), providing a first integrator voltage (VP) to a first integrator input (27) of a first integrator (26) by subtracting the first difference voltage (AVP) from a input common mode voltage (VINCM) and providing a second integrator voltage (VN) to a second integrator input (28) of the first integrator (26) by subtracting the second difference voltage (AVN) from the input common mode voltage (VINCM), and generating a digital output signal (DOUT) by digitizing an integrator difference voltage (AV) between the first integrator voltage (VP) and the second integrator voltage (VN). 16. Method according to claim 15, wherein a resistive voltage divider (85) generates the input common mode voltage (VINCM) out of the first and the second input voltage (VINP, VINN).
